description: Microsoft's flagship sci-fi military shooter franchise. Humanity versus the alien Covenant and the parasitic Flood, across ring-shaped superweapons built by an ancient civilization called the Forerunners. Spans two distinct creative eras: the Bungie era (2001–2010) and the 343 Industries era (2012–present), with differing foundational lore.

media.title: Halo: Combat Evolved
media.description: Master Chief discovers Installation 04 and the Flood. The original game that launched the franchise and defined console FPS controls.
media.notes: Play via MCC on PC/Xbox. Anniversary graphics toggle available.

media.title: The Fall of Reach
media.author: Eric Nylund
media.description: John-117's Spartan-II training and the fall of the planet Reach — the definitive Halo origin novel.
media.notes: Read before or alongside CE. Gold standard for Halo tie-in fiction.

media.title: The Flood
media.author: William C. Dietz
media.description: Novelization of Halo: Combat Evolved with expanded side-character content.
media.notes: Skippable if you've played CE. Completionists only.

media.title: First Strike
media.author: Eric Nylund
media.description: Bridge novel between CE and Halo 2 — Chief and Cortana fighting back toward Earth.
media.notes: Read after CE, before Halo 2.

media.title: Halo 2
media.description: Dual campaign — Master Chief and the Arbiter. Best Covenant worldbuilding in the series. Launched Xbox Live as a platform.
media.notes: Play via MCC. Anniversary CG cutscenes by Blur Studios.

media.title: Ghosts of Onyx
media.author: Eric Nylund
media.description: Spartan-IIIs, Dr. Halsey, and the Forerunner shield world Onyx — concurrent with Halo 2 and 3.
media.notes: Best Bungie-era novel after Fall of Reach. Devastating finale.

media.title: Contact Harvest
media.author: Joseph Staten
media.description: First Contact — how the Human-Covenant War began. Sergeant Johnson's origin story.
media.notes: Written by Bungie's own Joe Staten. Can be read at any point.

media.title: Halo 3
media.description: The Bungie trilogy conclusion — the Ark, the Gravemind's endgame, and Bungie's intended ending for Master Chief.
media.notes: Play via MCC. Read the in-game terminals for the Forerunner-as-human lore seeding.

media.title: Halo Legends
media.studio: Production I.G / Studio 4°C / Toei Animation / Blur Studio
media.description: Seven animated anthology shorts from Japanese and western studios — The Duel, Prototype, Origins, The Package, Homecoming, Babysitter, and Odd One Out.
media.notes: Canon-adjacent. Origins uses Bungie-era Forerunner framing that conflicts with later 343 retcons. The Duel and Prototype are standouts.

media.title: Halo 3: ODST
media.description: ODST squad during the Halo 2-era New Mombasa invasion — noir atmosphere, outstanding audio design, no Master Chief.
media.notes: Play via MCC. Best-paced Halo campaign.

media.title: Halo: Evolutions
media.author: Various
media.description: Multi-author short story anthology spanning the Human-Covenant War and beyond.
media.notes: Read selectively. Best stories: 'Midnight in the Heart of Midlothian' and 'The Mona Lisa'.

media.title: Halo: Reach
media.description: Bungie's final Halo — Noble Team's last stand on Reach, ending exactly where CE begins.
media.notes: Play last in the Bungie lineup for maximum emotional impact. Play via MCC.

media.title: Cryptum
media.author: Greg Bear
media.description: Forerunner Saga Book 1 — Forerunners established as a non-human alien species, directly contradicting Bungie's human-as-Forerunner lore seeding.
media.notes: Required before Halo 4. Accept the retcon or treat as an alternate branch.

media.title: Primordium
media.author: Greg Bear
media.description: Forerunner Saga Book 2 — ancient humans from a Forerunner POV, the Flood war escalating.
media.notes: Read after Cryptum, before Halo 4.

media.title: Halo 4
media.description: Master Chief returns — the Didact as antagonist, Cortana's rampancy arc, and 343's first mainline campaign.
media.notes: Play via MCC. Read Forerunner Saga first for full impact. Best 343 campaign.

media.title: Silentium
media.author: Greg Bear
media.description: Forerunner Saga Book 3 — the Flood endgame, the Librarian's sacrifice, and the firing of the Halo Array.
media.notes: The definitive retcon of Forerunner history. Read before Halo 4.

media.title: New Blood
media.author: Matt Forbeck
media.description: Buck's transition from ODST to Spartan-IV — bridges ODST to Halo 5.
media.notes: For ODST fans only.

media.title: Halo 5: Guardians
media.description: Split campaign between Blue Team and Fireteam Osiris — divisive story, strong multiplayer.
media.notes: Weakest main-series campaign. Relevant for Infinite context; a summary suffices.

media.title: Bad Blood
media.author: Matt Forbeck
media.description: Halo 5 aftermath — Buck and Alpha-Nine team aftermath.
media.notes: For ODST fans only.

media.title: Halo Infinite
media.description: Open-world Zeta Halo — the Banished as antagonists, the grappleshot, and Cortana's fallout.
media.notes: Campaign paid; multiplayer free-to-play on PC/Xbox. Best 343 game since Halo 4.

media.title: Halo: The Series — Season 1
media.description: Live-action series in the deliberate 'Silver Timeline' — a parallel canon separate from game lore. Master Chief has a personal arc and removes his helmet.
media.notes: Better than the discourse suggested. Approach as an elseworld, not a game adaptation.

media.title: Halo: The Series — Season 2
media.description: Season 2 of the Paramount+ series — covers the Fall of Reach arc, darker tone, better received than Season 1.
media.notes: Watch directly after Season 1.
